{{Quote|I just received a comm from ''Rafe's Gambit''. Stormtroopers are searching every ship.|Crys Taanzer|<ref name="DT:PTN">[[Dark Times 2|''Dark Times'' 2]]</ref>}}
'''''Rafe's Gambit''''' was an independent [[Transport/Legends|transport]] that became grounded on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[New Plympto/Legends|New Plympto]] around [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], following the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. While it was grounded, [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] boarded the [[Starship/Legends|starship]]. ''Rafe's Gambit'' later fled the planet after its crew was led to believe the [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial forces]] had been ordered to destroy the ship.

==Description==
The independent [[Transport/Legends|transport]] known as ''Rafe's Gambit'' was equipped with at least one [[Engine/Legends|engine]].<ref name="DT:PTN" />

==History==
[[File:Uhumele2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The crew of ''Rafe's Gambit'' contacted the crew of the ''Uhumele'' (pictured) during the Imperial search on New Plympto.]]
''Rafe's Gambit'' was based out of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[New Plympto/Legends|New Plympto]] in the aftermath of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].<ref name="Encyclopedia" /> Around the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[19 BBY]],<ref name="Date">[[Dark Times 2|''Dark Times'' 2]] is set approximately nineteen [[Standard year/Legends|years]] before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], which corresponds to [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> the [[Starship/Legends|starship]] landed in a [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] in an area of New Plympto known as [[Cadgel Meadows]]. While it was there, [[Imperial Military/Legends|forces]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] grounded all ships in the port, including ''Rafe's Gambit''.<ref name="DT:PTN" />

During ''Rafe's Gambit''{{'s}} wait for departure clearance,<ref name="DT:PTN" /> Imperial [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] boarded the vessel in search of fugitives. The crew of the ship then warned all other nearby vessels that the Imperials were boarding ships,<ref name="Encyclopedia" /> including the crew of the<ref name="DT:PTN" /> [[Maka-Eekai L4000 transport]]<ref name="FUCG">''[[The Force Unleashed Campaign Guide]]''</ref> ''[[Uhumele]]'', whom they reached by [[Comm unit/Legends|comm]]. [[Crys Taanzer]], [[Navigator/Legends|navigator]] of the ''Uhumele'', informed [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Schurk-Heren]] of the report from ''Rafe's Gambit''.<ref name="DT:PTN" />

The crew of the ''Uhumele'', also grounded in the port and not wanting to be searched, then had [[Dass Jennir|one]] of their number [[Thief/Legends|steal]] an Imperial [[Alpha-3 Nimbus-class V-wing starfighter/Legends|V-wing starfighter]] and begin firing on other ships in the port, making it appear as if the Imperials had orders to destroy all vessels grounded there. This resulted in all grounded ships in the port, including ''Rafe's Gambit'', taking off and fleeing the faked Imperial attack.<ref name="DT:PTN" />

==Behind the scenes==
''Rafe's Gambit'' was first mentioned in the [[Dark Times 2|second issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars: Dark Times]]'' [[Star Wars comic books|comic-book]] series as part of [[Star Wars: Dark Times: The Path to Nowhere|''The Path to Nowhere'']] arc. The issue was written by "Mick Harrison,"<ref name="DT:PTN" /> a pen name of author [[Randy Stradley]],<ref name="Insider 127">{{InsiderCite|127|Blaster}}</ref> and was released by [[Dark Horse Comics]] on [[November 29]], [[2006]].<ref name="Release Date">{{SWArchive|url=eu/lit/comics/news20060814.html|text=November 2006 Comics}}</ref>
